title: BlackBerry Stock Climbs As QNX And Cash Flow Strengthen
text: BlackBerry stock climbs as QNX and cash flow strengthen following its latest earnings report. BlackBerry generated operating cash flow of $46.1M and free cash flow of $44.4M in the latest quarter, demonstrating strong cash generation momentum. For full fiscal year 2026, BlackBerry achieved free cash flow of $46.5 million. BlackBerry's third-quarter fiscal year 2026 results marked a pivotal milestone: the company achieved positive operating and free cash flow for the first time in 12 quarters. This represents a significant turnaround after years of negative cash flow. Management is calling for about $100M in FY27 operating cash flow, indicating expectations for continued improvement in cash generation. The company's guidance for FY27 includes revenue climbing to the high-$500M to low-$600M range and operating cash flow around $100M. The balance sheet carries moderate leverage with total debt-to-equity at 0.29 and a current ratio of 2.1, providing financial flexibility. BlackBerry had total cash, cash equivalents, and investments of $432.4 million as of February 28, 2026.
